name: Stuffed Chicken Wings
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Nuoc Mam sauce or light soy sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the bones from the chicken wings using a sharp knife.
2. Gently separate the wingtip from the bone to retain the skin and a thin layer of meat.
3. Soak the vermicelli in warm water for 10 minutes, then drain well and cut into short strands.
4. Soak the wood ear fungus in warm water for 10 minutes, squeeze out excess moisture, and chop into thin slices.
5. Combine all stuffing ingredients in a bowl until firm.
6. Form the mixture into small balls and place them inside the cavity of each chicken wing.
7. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
8. Steam the stuffed wings for 10-15 minutes.
- For larger quantities, use more vermicelli, wood ear fungus, and pork, then freeze after steaming.
9. Transfer the cooked wings to a lightly oiled roasting pan.
10. Roast in the oven for an additional 30 minutes.
11. Serve on a bed of lettuce as an appetizer or alongside rice and a beef stir-fried dish.
